 {"id":"bd-2xm.2","title":"Implement rano (network observer) collector","description":"## Overview
Implement collector for rano - network activity observer for coding agents. This captures connections, domains, processes, and bandwidth so vc can surface anomalous traffic, auth loops, and provider-level issues.

## Integration Methods
- Primary: `rano export --format jsonl --since <duration>`
- Optional fallback: read `observer.sqlite` directly if export is unavailable
- Incremental cursor: track last event timestamp (and event_id if present) to avoid re-ingest

## DuckDB Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE net_events (
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    event_ts TIMESTAMP,
    provider TEXT,
    process_name TEXT,
    pid INTEGER,
    direction TEXT,          -- inbound | outbound
    protocol TEXT,           -- tcp | udp | https | ...
    remote_host TEXT,
    remote_ip TEXT,
    remote_port INTEGER,
    bytes_sent BIGINT,
    bytes_received BIGINT,
    tags_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, event_ts, process_name, remote_host)
);

CREATE TABLE net_session_summary (
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    provider TEXT,
    process_name TEXT,
    session_count INTEGER,
    bytes_sent BIGINT,
    bytes_received BIGINT,
    error_count INTEGER,
    top_remote_hosts_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, provider, process_name)
);

CREATE TABLE net_anomalies (
    machine_id TEXT,
    detected_at TIMESTAMP,
    anomaly_type TEXT,       -- unknown_provider | spike | auth_loop | burst
    severity TEXT,           -- info | warning | critical
    description TEXT,
    evidence_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, detected_at, anomaly_type)
);
```

## Collector Implementation
- Run export with a bounded window (default 10m) and parse JSONL line-by-line.
- Normalize provider/domain/process fields for stable aggregation.
- Store raw_json for forward compatibility.
- Fail-soft when rano is missing; mark collector_available=false and continue.

## Alert + UI Value
- Populate Events screen network section.
- Alert rules: unknown provider spike, auth-loop detection, bandwidth anomalies.

## Testing & Logging
Unit tests:
- Parse representative JSONL fixtures (happy path + malformed/partial lines).
- Normalize provider/domain/process fields.
- Error-path tests: missing binary, empty export, invalid JSONL.

Integration tests:
- Insert into DuckDB and query back net_events/net_session_summary.
- Cursor behavior: no duplicates across consecutive runs.

E2E:
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_rano_collect.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Script emits structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- JSON summary per run stored in `tests/logs/`.

## Remote Compatibility
- Collector must run locally and via SSH runner once bd-3nb.4 exists.
- Route execution through the CollectContext executor abstraction (local or remote).
- Add a remote-path test (localhost or mock) and hook into `tests/e2e/remote/test_remote_collectors.sh` (bd-30z).

 {"id":"bd-2xm.3","title":"Implement dcg (dangerous command guard) collector","description":"## Overview
Implement collector for dcg (Dangerous Command Guard) - tracks blocked dangerous commands and security events.

## Background
dcg intercepts and blocks dangerous shell commands (rm -rf /, git push --force, etc.). Tracking blocked commands helps understand agent behavior patterns and identify potential issues.

## Data Model (DuckDB Schema)
```sql
CREATE TABLE dcg_events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    machine_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    timestamp TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    event_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,          -- blocked, allowed_override, config_change
    command VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    working_dir VARCHAR,
    user VARCHAR,
    shell_pid INTEGER,
    parent_process VARCHAR,               -- claude-code, codex, bash, etc.
    rule_matched VARCHAR,                 -- Which dcg rule triggered
    severity VARCHAR,                     -- low, medium, high, critical
    override_reason VARCHAR,              -- If allowed after prompt
    session_id VARCHAR,                   -- Link to cass session if available
    collected_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE TABLE dcg_rules (
    rule_id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
    machine_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    pattern VARCHAR NOT NULL,             -- Regex or glob pattern
    action VARCHAR NOT NULL,              -- block, warn, log
    severity VARCHAR,
    description VARCHAR,
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now()
);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_dcg_ts ON dcg_events(timestamp);
CREATE INDEX idx_dcg_type ON dcg_events(event_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_dcg_severity ON dcg_events(severity);
```

## Ingestion Pattern: SQLite Incremental
dcg stores events in `~/.dcg/events.db`:
```sql
-- dcg native schema
CREATE TABLE events (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    ts TEXT,
    type TEXT,
    cmd TEXT,
    cwd TEXT,
    rule TEXT,
    severity TEXT,
    ...
);
```

## Collector Implementation
```rust
pub struct DcgCollector {
    db_path: PathBuf,
}

impl Collector for DcgCollector {
    const NAME: &str = "dcg";
    const TOOL_NAME: &str = "dcg";
    type Cursor = i64;  // Last event ID
    
    async fn collect(&self, ctx: &CollectContext) -> Result<CollectResult> {
        let conn = rusqlite::Connection::open(&self.db_path)?;
        let last_id = ctx.cursor.unwrap_or(0);
        
        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(
            "SELECT id, ts, type, cmd, cwd, rule, severity, override_reason
             FROM events 
             WHERE id > ?
             ORDER BY id"
        )?;
        
        let events: Vec<DcgEvent> = stmt.query_map([last_id], |row| {
            Ok(DcgEvent {
                id: row.get(0)?,
                timestamp: row.get(1)?,
                event_type: row.get(2)?,
                command: row.get(3)?,
                working_dir: row.get(4)?,
                rule_matched: row.get(5)?,
                severity: row.get(6)?,
                override_reason: row.get(7)?,
            })
        })?.collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?;
        
        let new_cursor = events.last().map(|e| e.id).unwrap_or(last_id);
        
        Ok(CollectResult {
            table: "dcg_events",
            records: events.into_iter().map(|e| e.to_row()).collect(),
            new_cursor: Some(Cursor::Integer(new_cursor)),
            machine_id: ctx.machine_id.clone(),
        })
    }
}
```

## Key Metrics & Queries
```sql
-- Blocked commands by severity (last 24h)
SELECT severity, 
       COUNT(*) as count,
       array_agg(DISTINCT rule_matched) as rules
FROM dcg_events
WHERE event_type = "blocked"
  AND timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 1 DAY
GROUP BY severity
ORDER BY 
    CASE severity WHEN "critical" THEN 1 WHEN "high" THEN 2 WHEN "medium" THEN 3 ELSE 4 END;

-- Most frequently blocked commands
SELECT command, 
       COUNT(*) as block_count,
       MIN(timestamp) as first_blocked,
       MAX(timestamp) as last_blocked
FROM dcg_events
WHERE event_type = "blocked"
GROUP BY command
ORDER BY block_count DESC
LIMIT 20;

-- Blocks by parent process (agent detection)
SELECT parent_process,
       COUNT(*) as blocks,
       COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) as sessions
FROM dcg_events
WHERE event_type = "blocked"
  AND timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 7 DAY
GROUP BY parent_process
ORDER BY blocks DESC;

-- Override patterns (which dangerous commands were allowed)
SELECT command, override_reason, COUNT(*) as times
FROM dcg_events
WHERE event_type = "allowed_override"
GROUP BY command, override_reason
ORDER BY times DESC;
```

## Guardian Integration
dcg events can trigger Guardian alerts:
```rust
// In vc_guardian
impl GuardianRule for DcgCriticalRule {
    fn check(&self, store: &VcStore) -> Option<Alert> {
        let critical_count = store.query(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dcg_events 
             WHERE severity = \"critical\" 
             AND timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 5 MINUTE"
        )?;
        
        if critical_count > 0 {
            Some(Alert::new(
                AlertLevel::Critical,
                "Critical command blocked by dcg",
                "Check dcg_events for details"
            ))
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

- [ ] Works on local and remote machines

 {"id":"bd-2xm.4","title":"Implement pt (process tracker) collector","description":"## Overview
Implement collector for pt (Process Tracker) - tracks long-running processes and their resource usage.

## Background
pt monitors running processes, especially agent sessions and build processes. It provides process lifecycle events, resource usage over time, and parent-child relationships.

## Data Model (DuckDB Schema)
```sql
CREATE TABLE pt_processes (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    machine_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    pid INTEGER NOT NULL,
    ppid INTEGER,                         -- Parent PID
    name VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    cmdline VARCHAR,
    user VARCHAR,
    started_at TIMESTAMP,
    ended_at TIMESTAMP,
    exit_code INTEGER,
    cpu_time_ms BIGINT,
    peak_memory_mb INTEGER,
    status VARCHAR,                       -- running, stopped, zombie
    category VARCHAR,                     -- agent, build, system, other
    session_id VARCHAR,                   -- Link to cass session
    collected_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (machine_id, pid, started_at)
);

CREATE TABLE pt_snapshots (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    machine_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    pid INTEGER NOT NULL,
    snapshot_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    cpu_percent FLOAT,
    memory_mb FLOAT,
    memory_percent FLOAT,
    threads INTEGER,
    open_files INTEGER,
    io_read_bytes BIGINT,
    io_write_bytes BIGINT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now()
);

-- Process tree relationships
CREATE TABLE pt_tree (
    machine_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    parent_pid INTEGER NOT NULL,
    child_pid INTEGER NOT NULL,
    depth INTEGER,                        -- Distance from root
    collected_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now(),
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, parent_pid, child_pid)
);

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_pt_proc_machine ON pt_processes(machine_id, started_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_pt_proc_category ON pt_processes(category);
CREATE INDEX idx_pt_snap_ts ON pt_snapshots(snapshot_at);
```

## Ingestion Pattern: CLI Incremental Window
pt provides snapshots via `pt list --robot --json --since <timestamp>`:
```json
{
  "processes": [
    {
      "pid": 12345,
      "ppid": 1234,
      "name": "claude-code",
      "cmdline": "claude-code --project /data/projects/vc",
      "started_at": "2025-01-27T10:00:00Z",
      "cpu_percent": 45.2,
      "memory_mb": 512.3,
      "category": "agent"
    }
  ],
  "ended": [
    {"pid": 12340, "exit_code": 0, "ended_at": "2025-01-27T10:05:00Z"}
  ]
}
```

## Collector Implementation
```rust
pub struct PtCollector;

impl Collector for PtCollector {
    const NAME: &str = "pt";
    const TOOL_NAME: &str = "pt";
    type Cursor = DateTime<Utc>;
    
    async fn collect(&self, ctx: &CollectContext) -> Result<CollectResult> {
        let since = ctx.cursor
            .unwrap_or_else(|| Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::hours(1));
        
        let output = Command::new("pt")
            .args(["list", "--robot", "--json", "--since", &since.to_rfc3339()])
            .output()
            .await?;
        
        let data: PtOutput = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout)?;
        
        let mut result = CollectResult::new();
        
        // Active processes - upsert
        for proc in data.processes {
            result.add_rows("pt_processes", vec![proc.to_row()]);
            
            // Snapshot current resource usage
            result.add_rows("pt_snapshots", vec![
                row!(
                    machine_id = ctx.machine_id,
                    pid = proc.pid,
                    snapshot_at = Utc::now(),
                    cpu_percent = proc.cpu_percent,
                    memory_mb = proc.memory_mb,
                    // ...
                )
            ]);
        }
        
        // Ended processes - update
        for ended in data.ended {
            result.add_update("pt_processes",
                Update::set("ended_at", ended.ended_at)
                    .set("exit_code", ended.exit_code)
                    .where_eq("machine_id", ctx.machine_id)
                    .where_eq("pid", ended.pid)
            );
        }
        
        result.new_cursor = Some(Cursor::Timestamp(Utc::now()));
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## Process Categorization
```rust
fn categorize_process(name: &str, cmdline: &str) -> &str {
    if name.contains("claude") || name.contains("codex") || name.contains("gemini") {
        "agent"
    } else if name.contains("cargo") || name.contains("rustc") || name.contains("npm") {
        "build"
    } else if name.contains("node") || name.contains("python") || name.contains("ruby") {
        "runtime"
    } else {
        "other"
    }
}
```

## Key Queries
```sql
-- Active agent processes
SELECT machine_id, pid, name, cmdline, 
       started_at,
       age(now(), started_at) as runtime
FROM pt_processes
WHERE category = "agent"
  AND ended_at IS NULL
ORDER BY started_at DESC;

-- Resource usage over time for a process
SELECT snapshot_at, cpu_percent, memory_mb
FROM pt_snapshots
WHERE machine_id = ? AND pid = ?
ORDER BY snapshot_at;

-- Process tree for an agent
WITH RECURSIVE tree AS (
    SELECT pid, name, 0 as depth
    FROM pt_processes WHERE pid = ?
    UNION ALL
    SELECT p.pid, p.name, t.depth + 1
    FROM pt_processes p
    JOIN tree t ON p.ppid = t.pid
)
SELECT * FROM tree ORDER BY depth, pid;

-- Processes by resource usage
SELECT machine_id, pid, name, 
       MAX(cpu_percent) as peak_cpu,
       MAX(memory_mb) as peak_mem
FROM pt_snapshots
WHERE snapshot_at > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR
GROUP BY machine_id, pid, name
ORDER BY peak_cpu DESC
LIMIT 20;
```

-{"id":"bd-2xm.5","title":"Implement Alerts MVP","description":"## Overview
Build the alerting system that monitors collected data and triggers notifications when conditions are met.

## Background
With multiple collectors gathering data, we need an alerting system to notify users of important events: rate limits approaching, critical commands blocked, agents stuck, etc.

## Architecture
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   AlertEngine                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Rules[]     → Check conditions against store        │
│  History[]   → Track fired alerts (dedup)           │
│  Channels[]  → Deliver via TUI/webhook/desktop      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Data Model (DuckDB Schema)
```sql
CREATE TABLE alert_rules (
    rule_id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    description VARCHAR,
    severity VARCHAR NOT NULL,            -- info, warning, critical
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    check_interval_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
    condition_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,      -- threshold, pattern, absence
    condition_config JSON NOT NULL,       -- Rule-specific params
    cooldown_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 300, -- Dedup window
    channels VARCHAR[],                   -- Where to send
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE alert_history (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    rule_id VARCHAR REFERENCES alert_rules(rule_id),
    fired_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    resolved_at TIMESTAMP,
    severity VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    title VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    message TEXT,
    context JSON,                         -- Additional data
    machine_id VARCHAR,
    acknowledged BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    acknowledged_by VARCHAR,
    acknowledged_at TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE alert_channels (
    channel_id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
    channel_type VARCHAR NOT NULL,        -- tui, webhook, desktop, email
    config JSON NOT NULL,                 -- Type-specific config
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE
);
```

## Alert Rule Types
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum AlertCondition {
    /// Fire when metric exceeds threshold
    Threshold {
        query: String,           // SQL returning single value
        operator: ThresholdOp,   // gt, gte, lt, lte, eq
        value: f64,
    },
    
    /// Fire when pattern appears in data
    Pattern {
        table: String,
        column: String,
        regex: String,
    },
    
    /// Fire when expected data is missing
    Absence {
        table: String,
        max_age_seconds: u64,
    },
    
    /// Fire on rate of change
    RateOfChange {
        query: String,
        window_seconds: u64,
        threshold_per_second: f64,
    },
}
```

## Alert Engine Implementation
```rust
pub struct AlertEngine {
    store: Arc<VcStore>,
    rules: Vec<AlertRule>,
    history: DashMap<String, Instant>,  // Cooldown tracking
}

impl AlertEngine {
    pub async fn check_all(&self) -> Vec<Alert> {
        let mut alerts = Vec::new();
        
        for rule in &self.rules {
            if !rule.enabled {
                continue;
            }
            
            // Check cooldown
            if let Some(last_fired) = self.history.get(&rule.rule_id) {
                if last_fired.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(rule.cooldown_seconds) {
                    continue;
                }
            }
            
            // Evaluate condition
            if let Some(alert) = self.evaluate_rule(rule).await? {
                self.history.insert(rule.rule_id.clone(), Instant::now());
                self.store.record_alert(&alert).await?;
                alerts.push(alert);
            }
        }
        
        alerts
    }
    
    async fn evaluate_rule(&self, rule: &AlertRule) -> Result<Option<Alert>> {
        match &rule.condition {
            AlertCondition::Threshold { query, operator, value } => {
                let result: f64 = self.store.query_scalar(query).await?;
                if operator.check(result, *value) {
                    Some(Alert::from_rule(rule, format!("Value {} {} threshold {}", result, operator, value)))
                } else {
                    None
                }
            }
            // ... other condition types
        }
    }
}
```

## Delivery Channels
```rust
pub trait AlertChannel: Send + Sync {
    async fn deliver(&self, alert: &Alert) -> Result<()>;
}

pub struct TuiChannel {
    tx: mpsc::Sender<Alert>,
}

pub struct WebhookChannel {
    url: String,
    client: reqwest::Client,
}

pub struct DesktopChannel;  // Uses notify-rust

impl AlertChannel for WebhookChannel {
    async fn deliver(&self, alert: &Alert) -> Result<()> {
        self.client.post(&self.url)
            .json(alert)
            .send()
            .await?;
        Ok(())
```

## Alert Engine Implementation
```rust
pub struct AlertEngine {
    store: Arc<VcStore>,
    rules: Vec<AlertRule>,
    history: DashMap<String, Instant>,  // Cooldown tracking
}

impl AlertEngine {
    pub async fn check_all(&self) -> Vec<Alert> {
        let mut alerts = Vec::new();
        
        for rule in &self.rules {
            if !rule.enabled {
                continue;
            }
            
            // Check cooldown
            if let Some(last_fired) = self.history.get(&rule.rule_id) {
                if last_fired.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(rule.cooldown_seconds) {
                    continue;
                }
            }
            
            // Evaluate condition
            if let Some(alert) = self.evaluate_rule(rule).await? {
                self.history.insert(rule.rule_id.clone(), Instant::now());
                self.store.record_alert(&alert).await?;
                alerts.push(alert);
            }
        }
        
        alerts
    }
    
    async fn evaluate_rule(&self, rule: &AlertRule) -> Result<Option<Alert>> {
        match &rule.condition {
            AlertCondition::Threshold { query, operator, value } => {
                let result: f64 = self.store.query_scalar(query).await?;
                if operator.check(result, *value) {
                    Some(Alert::from_rule(rule, format!("Value {} {} threshold {}", result, operator, value)))
                } else {
                    None
                }
            }
            // ... other condition types
        }
    }
}
```

```rust
        allow_failure: bool,

    /// Send notification

        message: String,
    },
    
    /// Wait/delay
    Wait { seconds: u64 },
    
    /// Conditional branch
    Condition {
        query: String,
        then_steps: Vec<PlaybookStep>,
        else_steps: Vec<PlaybookStep>,
    },
}
```

## Guardian Executor
```rust
pub struct Guardian {
    store: Arc<VcStore>,
    playbooks: Vec<Playbook>,
    alert_engine: Arc<AlertEngine>,
}

impl Guardian {
    pub async fn run_loop(&self) {
        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(30));
        
        loop {
            interval.tick().await;
            
            // Check alerts
            let alerts = self.alert_engine.check_all().await;
            
            // Find triggered playbooks
            for alert in alerts {
                for playbook in &self.playbooks {
                    if playbook.should_trigger(&alert) {
                        if let Err(e) = self.execute_playbook(playbook, &alert).await {
                            tracing::error!("Playbook {} failed: {}", playbook.playbook_id, e);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    async fn execute_playbook(&self, playbook: &Playbook, context: &Alert) -> Result<()> {
        // Check rate limit
        let recent_runs = self.store.count_recent_runs(
            &playbook.playbook_id,
            Duration::from_secs(3600)
        ).await?;
        
        if recent_runs >= playbook.max_runs_per_hour {
            tracing::warn!("Playbook {} rate limited", playbook.playbook_id);
            return Ok(());
        }
        
        // Check approval requirement
        if playbook.requires_approval {
            // Queue for approval, dont execute immediately
            self.store.queue_pending_playbook(playbook, context).await?;
            return Ok(());
        }
        
        // Execute steps
        let run_id = self.store.create_run(playbook, context).await?;
        
        for (i, step) in playbook.steps.iter().enumerate() {
            let result = self.execute_step(step, context).await;
            self.store.log_step(run_id, i, &result).await?;
            
            if result.is_err() && !step.allow_failure() {
                self.store.mark_run_failed(run_id, &result).await?;
                return result;
            }
        }
        
        self.store.mark_run_success(run_id).await?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

```
+------------------------------------------------+
| PENDING INTERVENTIONS                           |

| │   └─ Waiting for: manual approval             |
```

```rust
fn severity_color(severity: &Severity) -> Color {
    match severity {
        Severity::Critical => Color::Red,
        Severity::High => Color::LightRed,
        Severity::Warning => Color::Yellow,
        Severity::Info => Color::Blue,
        Severity::Low => Color::Gray,
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-2xy","title":"Implement vc watch streaming mode","description":"Implement vc watch command for real-time JSONL event streaming.

PURPOSE:
Enable guardian agents to monitor without polling full snapshots. Events are emitted on change or at fixed intervals.

COMMAND USAGE:
vc watch --format jsonl --interval 30
vc watch --format jsonl --changes-only
vc watch --format toon --interval 60

EVENT TYPES:
- alert: New or escalated alerts
  {"type":"alert","ts":"...","alert_id":"...","severity":"critical","machine":"orko","message":"..."}

- prediction: Forecast risk crossing threshold
  {"type":"prediction","ts":"...","prediction_type":"rate_limit","machine":"orko","confidence":0.85,"action":"swap_now"}

- opportunity: Optimization hints
  {"type":"opportunity","ts":"...","opportunity_type":"cost_saving","estimated_savings":12.50,"action":"..."}

- health_change: Machine/fleet health state change
  {"type":"health_change","ts":"...","machine":"orko","old_score":0.85,"new_score":0.62,"factor":"cpu_high"}

- collector_status: Collector success/failure
  {"type":"collector_status","ts":"...","collector":"sysmoni","machine":"orko","status":"ok","duration_ms":234}

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Create vc_cli/src/watch.rs
- Use tokio broadcast channel for event distribution
- Support filtering by event type: --events alert,prediction
- Support filtering by machine: --machines orko,sydneymc
- Support severity threshold: --min-severity high

OUTPUT OPTIONS:
- --format jsonl: One JSON object per line (default)
- --format toon: Token-optimized format
- --interval N: Emit summary every N seconds even if no changes
- --changes-only: Only emit when something changes
- --buffer N: Buffer up to N events before emitting (batch mode)

GRACEFUL HANDLING:
- Reconnect to daemon if connection lost
- Emit heartbeat events periodically
- Clean shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM

TESTS:
- Event emission tests
- Filter application tests
- Format output tests
- Reconnection tests

## E2E & Logging
- Add `tests/e2e/cli/test_watch_stream.sh` to validate JSONL output, filters, and heartbeat handling.
- Capture stdout/stderr and emit JSON summary in tests/logs/.
